public_reputation: Formalized corpse stewardship so the dead could be handled lawfully without civic desecration.
appearance: Work-worn layers, repair marks, and tools or soot traces show responsibility for systems that fail loudly when neglected.
manner: Views problems as maintenance loads, throughput failures, or structural weaknesses before anything else. In Panim, that reads as someone grave, measured, and ritual-aware, with constant awareness of consequence, grief, and divine mediation, and the habits of a necromancers still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of necromancers in and around Panim Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Panim Haven workable through the obligations of necromancers while managing the immediate pressure of divine balance obligations in Panim.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the underlying system working while everyone notices it only when stress, wear, or neglect begin to show. Around Panim Haven, that usually means carrying divine balance obligations, sacrificial and mediation decorum, institutional scrutiny without letting the role of necromancers visibly slip.

support_scope_notes: Integrated from the prior_release canon-ready named-NPC ledger append. Unsupported fields remain restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Tool wear, work scars, or system-focused habits make Ossarch Veyn Halcyon visibly responsible for keeping something larger from failing. In Panim Haven, this lands inside a public field already shaped by House Panim.
